conclusion: Five current post-c9 grouped-stress cycles did not reproduce the historical explicit c2 batched_lm_head flake: each known-red c9 run was followed by a c2 explicit batched_lm_head/no-audit run with [137,137] generated-token equality, sampler mode batched_lm_head, batch argmax, native_row_aware_lm_head=true, and empty sampler blockers. This strengthens the evidence that the historical [103,137] path is timing/transient rather than deterministic, but it is not enough to restore c2 batched_lm_head as the default: iter222 remains the contrary flake artifact, and the active c2 default stays serial_lm_head until the flake is reproduced under audit or a stronger burn-in policy is accepted. No throughput claim is made.
remaining_blockers: explicit c2 batched_lm_head still has historical post-c9 flake evidence from iter222 despite non-reproduction in this five-cycle current rerun
c2 default remains serial_lm_head until a stronger decision is made
c4/c8 full-native/no-rowchunk grouped full-attention remains red and blocks retained c4/c8 throughput claims
c9 grouped-compact MoE row-count boundary remains separate and red
